scope_note: QA Pythagorean H,I Median Identity cert [339]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1993) Pythagorean Arithmetic Vol I, pp.12, 32-33: 'the values of H and I are such that H^2+I^2=2G^2. This may seem trivial in that this equality can be easily derived from C^2+F^2=G^2 or (C+F)^2+(C-F)^2=2G^2.' And: 'the sum and difference of the altitude and base are functionally prime numbers, H and I.' (C1) H=C+F and I=|C-F| verified for 11 pairs. (C2) H^2+I^2=2G^2 (G is median: G^2=(H^2+I^2)/2) verified for 12 pairs. (C3) {H+I, H-I}={2C, 2F}: H+I=2*max(C,F) and H-I=2*min(C,F). (C4) Algebraic proof: (C+F)^2+(C-F)^2=2(C^2+F^2)=2G^2 for all coprime pairs b,e<12. (C5) gcd(H,G)=1 and gcd(I,G)=1 (H,I coprime to hypotenuse G) for 12 pairs. DOI: primary text, no external DOI.
